commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed for the credential-import UX, public SDK surface, and contributor proof gate before any repair automation would be appropriate.; Needs attention: The diff handles sensitive credential import and has a concrete flag-precedence issue that can ignore an explicit auth opt-out.; Review finding: [P1] Honor --no-auth-credentials when secrets are included — src/commands/migrate.ts:72-73 (sha=ffd1e6e6df6069b902776341eec4bd63c0169004)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds interactive `openclaw migrate` auth credential prompting, Codex and Hermes auth-profile/config imports, migration conflict guards, docs updates, and a provider-auth SDK helper export.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: pass both `--include-secrets` and `--no-auth-credentials`, and the new resolver keeps `includeSecrets: true` before checking the opt-out. I did not run the CLI because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed for the credential-import UX, public SDK surface, and contributor proof gate before any repair automation would be appropriate.

target.title: Migrate auth credentials from Codex and Hermes
target.body:
## Summary

- Add interactive `openclaw migrate` credential prompting with yes selected by default, while keeping non-interactive `--yes` credential import explicit via `--include-secrets` and adding `--no-auth-credentials`.
- Migrate Codex and Hermes OAuth/API-key credentials into auth profiles and config, seed usable OpenAI model config from imported Codex auth, and avoid archiving raw Hermes `auth.json`.
- Add conflict and TOCTOU guards for auth profile store/config writes, including multi-account Hermes credential-pool imports.
- Update migration docs and plugin SDK API baseline for the new auth config helper export.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/files-and-skills.test.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/commands/migrate/output.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `git diff --check`
- Previous pre-push proof also passed: `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`, `pnpm format:docs -- docs/install/migrating-hermes.md`, `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw migrate` now prompts interactively for auth credential migration, imports supported Codex/Hermes OAuth and API-key credentials when selected, and configures usable auth/model defaults without silently overwriting existing auth config.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI from this branch, using `node openclaw.mjs` with a disposable `OPENCLAW_HOME`/`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and a synthetic Hermes source containing `config.yaml` plus a fake `OPENAI_API_KEY` in `.env`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/hermes --include-secrets --yes --no-backup --force --json`, followed by reading the generated OpenClaw `auth-profiles.json` and `openclaw.json` from the disposable state directory.
Evidence after fix: Real CLI terminal output, with the synthetic key redacted:

```json
{
  "providerId": "hermes",
  "summary": {
    "total": 3,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 3,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "migratedItems": [
    { "id": "config:default-model", "kind": "config", "sensitive": false },
    { "id": "config:model-providers", "kind": "config", "sensitive": false },
    { "id": "secret:openai", "kind": "secret", "sensitive": true }
  ],
  "authProfile": {
    "type": "api_key",
    "provider": "openai",
    "key": "<redacted synthetic key>",
    "displayName": "Hermes import"
  },
  "defaultModel": "openai/gpt-5.4",
  "modelProviderApiKey": {
    "source": "env",
    "provider": "default",
    "id": "OPENAI_API_KEY"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: The migration imported the Hermes API key into an OpenClaw auth profile, configured the OpenAI model provider to use that key, set the migrated default model, and finished with zero errors or conflicts.
What was not tested: Live third-party OAuth refresh against real external accounts and broad CI/Testbox lanes.
